items.title: Partial derivatives
items.description: Jacobian and Hessian entries are partial derivatives—first order for J, second order for H.

items.title: Gradients
items.description: For a scalar function, the gradient is the transpose of the 1×n Jacobian; both collect first partial derivatives.

items.title: Chain rule
items.description: Composed maps multiply Jacobians: J_{h∘f} = J_h · J_f—the multivariable rule behind backpropagation.

items.title: Gradient descent
items.description: First-order optimization uses the gradient; Hessian-based methods extend the same loss surface with curvature-aware steps.

items.title: Loss surfaces
items.description: The Hessian describes local shape of a loss surface over parameters—bowls, ridges, and saddles at critical points.

items.title: Eigenvalues of the Hessian
items.description: Hessian eigenvalues classify curvature directions at a critical point, paralleling eigenanalysis of linear maps.
